classObjective: Model real-world situations with linear equations in slope-intercept form, interpret the meaning of slope and intercept in context, and graph linear functions.
workedExamples: 4-3, Example 3 — Model Real-World Situations with Linear Equations
4-3, Example 4 — Graph from Slope and y-Intercept
4-3, Example 5 — Model a Streaming Plan Situation
4-3, Example 6 — Equation, Graph, and Evaluation for a Savings Context
warmUp: A gym charges $50 membership plus $3 per class — write the equation
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: identifying b (starting value) and m (rate) from verbal descriptions; streaming plan and savings contexts; connecting equation to graph
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice modeling real-world situations with linear equations and interpreting slope/intercept
independentPractice: Practice problems writing, graphing, and interpreting linear models in context
assessment: Exit ticket: write and graph a linear model for a taxi fare with $4.50 base and $2.30 per mile
capReflection: How do you identify which part of a real-world situation is the slope and which is the y-intercept?
